brief_document.title: Scaffold Rust SDK crate with error taxonomy and public API re-exports
brief_document.purpose: Bootstrap the agora-sdk Rust crate with its Cargo.toml, public module structure, and error taxonomy. This is the foundation crate for the embedded-first SDK (ADR-010) that all subsequent Rust SDK briefs build on. The error type (SdkError) establishes the vocabulary every SDK operation uses to report failures.
brief_document.task: Create the crates/agora-sdk crate scaffold. Cargo.toml declares dependencies on agora core and protocol crates. lib.rs defines the public module tree and re-exports the key types. error.rs defines the SdkError enum using thiserror with variants covering connection, protocol, serialisation, type validation, backpressure, and conversation errors. IN SCOPE: crate manifest, module structure, error taxonomy, re-exports. OUT OF SCOPE: channel logic, conversation logic, connection management, transport implementation.

requirements.title: Create Cargo.toml with workspace and dependency declarations
requirements.spec: The agora-sdk crate manifest SHALL declare edition 2021, SHALL depend on agora-core and agora-protocol as workspace dependencies, SHALL depend on thiserror for error derivation, serde for serialisation traits, and bytes for zero-copy buffer handling. The crate SHALL be added to the workspace members list.
acceptance: cargo check -p agora-sdk succeeds with no errors
Cargo.toml [dependencies] includes agora-core, agora-protocol, thiserror, serde, and bytes
The workspace root Cargo.toml lists agora-sdk in its members array

requirements.title: Define lib.rs with public module structure and re-exports
requirements.spec: lib.rs SHALL declare pub mod for channel, conversation, types, pressure, connection, error, embedded, and remote modules. lib.rs SHALL re-export SdkError as the crate-level error type. lib.rs SHALL contain only module declarations and pub use re-exports, no logic (constraint CN3).
acceptance: lib.rs contains pub mod declarations for all planned SDK modules
pub use error::SdkError is present at crate root
lib.rs contains zero function definitions and zero impl blocks

requirements.title: Define SdkError enum with thiserror derivation
requirements.spec: error.rs SHALL define an SdkError enum deriving thiserror::Error and Debug. The enum SHALL include variants: Connection (with source from connection errors), Protocol (with source from protocol errors), Serialization (with source from serde errors), TypeValidation (with description string), Backpressure (with reason string), Conversation (with conversation id and description), and Store (with source from storage errors). Each variant SHALL carry enough context for the caller to distinguish failure modes without downcasting.
acceptance: SdkError has at least 7 variants: Connection, Protocol, Serialization, TypeValidation, Backpressure, Conversation, Store
Each variant's Display output includes the variant-specific context (e.g. conversation id for Conversation)
SdkError implements std::error::Error via thiserror derivation
No variant uses String as its sole payload without a distinguishing field name

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement channel publish, subscribe, or request-reply logic.
SHALL NOT implement conversation send, receive, or lifecycle logic.
SHALL NOT add any network transport or connection management.
SHALL NOT define the SchemaValidate trait (deferred to SDK-002).
SHALL NOT implement backpressure response types (deferred to SDK-003).
verification: cargo clippy -p agora-sdk -- -D warnings passes with no diagnostics
cargo fmt -p agora-sdk --check reports no formatting issues
cargo doc -p agora-sdk --no-deps generates documentation without warnings
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding comments and whitespace)

adrs.title: Embedded-first architecture with standalone mode as a deployment option
adrs.decision: The bus is a Rust library (crate) first, a standalone binary second. The embedded story (Aion workflows and bus channels in the same beamr instance) is the primary use case. Standalone mode is the same code with a network listener. The rejected alternative is standalone-first (adds a deployment dependency and network hop for every message, negating the beamr advantage).

checklist.text: agora-sdk crate exists at crates/agora-sdk/ with Cargo.toml declaring dependencies on agora core and protocol crates.

constraints.text: No file over 500 lines. If a module approaches this limit, extract into submodules.
No panics in production code. .unwrap() and .expect() only in tests.
mod.rs contains ONLY pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports. No logic.
All public types implement Debug. All error types implement std::error::Error.
The Rust SDK must compile with no_std + alloc for WASM targets.
Backpressure responses must never be silently consumed by the SDK. Every pressure signal reaches the application.
Reconnection attempts use exponential backoff with jitter. No fixed-interval retry loops.
The TypeScript SDK must work in both browser (WASM) and server (Node.js) environments without conditional imports in application code.

resolved_context.intention:
When this cluster is complete, developers in three ecosystems can connect to agora and use its full feature set — channels, conversations, typed messages, backpressure — with APIs that feel native to each language. The Rust SDK is the primary interface for embedded use: Aion workflows, co-located services, and any Rust application that wants zero-hop messaging. The Gleam SDK is the authoring convenience layer: message types defined in Gleam get compile-time enforcement, and conversation handlers are authored as Gleam modules running in beamr. The TypeScript SDK serves browser WASM and server-side Node.js, bringing agora to web applications and the JavaScript ecosystem.

The experience should be: import the SDK, connect (or embed), and start conversing. Connection management, reconnection, subscription state recovery, and backpressure handling are SDK responsibilities — application code deals with messages and conversations, not transport.
